dialogos.texto_en: Are those two over there Raiha and Yotsuba?\nWhat are they looking at?
Isn't that a traditional Okinawan costume?\nHey, let's take a closer look too!
Yo, what are you two looking at?
Ah, big bro.
That outfit on display in the shop window\nis a traditional Okinawan costume.
I thought so. What was it called again?
Ryusou!\nIt's a garment that was worn by kings and nobles\nduring the Ryukyu Kingdom era.
Wow, it's a really gorgeous costume.\nI bet the colors and patterns\nhave some special meanings too.
Oh, you're spot on, Uesugi-san!\nThe colors of the garment indicate\nthe wearer's social status.
Ehehe.\nActually, just a moment ago, a shop staff member\ntalked to me a little about Ryusou%p-2;――
I was itching to tell someone,\nso it's good timing\nthat I can tell you two!
Okay okay, go ahead and tell me.
First, it's about the patterns on kimono.\nFor women's kimono, they often use a type of dyeing\ncalled bingata that features natural motifs.
I see. The patterns are inspired\nby flowers and landscapes.
They're all so vivid and beautiful.\nJust looking at them makes you happy.
Also, with ryusou, you can have fun playing with\nthe color combinations of the inner and outer layers.\nLike pairing red and blue, or yellow and black.
Color combinations, huh...\nIn other words, the wearer's sense of style\nis being put to the test.
Hey, Futaro.\nWhat color do you think would look good on me?
What color would look good on you?\nWell, I think any color would.
What? Is something wrong?
Big bro.\nYou're being asked a question.\nYou need to give a proper answer.
I did answer.
Your answer was way too vague!
It's fine, Raiha-chan.\nIt's Futaro being Futaro. I could have expected that reply.
(I was serious when I said she'd look good in anything,\n but even Nino helped me out here...)
Umm...
If I had to say, when it comes to things like kimono,\nNino often wears black or purple!
Yeah, I suppose.\nThose colors suit me well.
Yeah, you look good in black and purple, Nino!
(Is that so?\n I'll remember that.)
Hm.\nThey have something black over there.
Ah, that's for men.
It would look good on you, Futaro.
I wonder.\nI can't quite picture it.
It'll totally suit you.\nI bet you'd look good in something more stylish.
Stylish, huh...
Seems like you still don't get it.
Well, Uesugi-san is a fashion amateur, after all.
Sh-shut up, Yotsuba!
Come to think of it,\ndo you have a favorite color, Futaro?
Not particularly.
I'll be matching with Nino.
Huh?
Well, you like black too, right?
Teehee, that's right.\nMatching is good, but%p-2;――
In that case, we shouldn't necessarily choose black,\nbut colors that complement each other\nand look good side by side.
Don't you think it's better for us to stand out\nindividually rather than just matching each other?
Indeed.\nThat opinion is so typical of you, Nino.
Right?
Come to think of it,\ndo you have a favorite color, Futaro?
Not particularly.
Well... I'd wear it at your recommendation.
Really?!\nThen, why don't we try them on together?
WHA%p-2;――%p;?!\nRight here, right now?
Right here.\nActually, there won't be another chance to wear it,\ndon't you think?
Th-that's true.
This shop rents them out, right?\nLet's ask!
Umm, Nino?\nSorry to rain on your parade, but%p-2;――
It's by reservation only,\nand they're not accepting any more today.
Ehhh?
What the hell.\nI wanted to try them on with Futaro.
Well, things like this are popular among tourists.\nThere's nothing we can do about it.
I see, I should have looked it up properly.
We can try it again\nwhen we come back next time, Futaro.
Y-yeah.
Come to think of it,\ndo you have a favorite color, Futaro?
Not particularly.
Quick answer...
But, if you don't have a favorite color,\nit just means there are more things\nto look forward to, right?
Wh-what's with that smile?\nWhat do you mean?
I'll be coordinating your outfits from now on.\nLet's find your favorite color together.
Oh, I see.
Does it mean that Nino-san is going to dye you in her colors,\nbig bro?
What?!
Teehee, I guess you could say that, hm?
Hey, which is better, you dyeing me,\nor me dyeing you, Futaro... Which do you prefer?
This means... Uesugi-san, you have to try harder!
(Exactly.\n Nino seems to be having fun, but\n you can hardly say that I'm leading her.)
(This is a more vexing problem\n than I thought...)
